§CShow Gamerules Decisions§!: These decisions will disappear after 1836.\n\n§RIn Multiplayer, these decisions should only be touched by the host.§!
This will enable crises from the start of the game.\n\n§RThis is mutually exclusive with delayed crises (below).§!
This will enable crises starting from the year 1870.\n\n§RThis is mutually exclusive with immediate crises (above).§!
This will enable the dismantle casus belli.

This will enable manual economic control, allowing all players to build and manage factories, build railroads, and invest in pop projects regardless of their ruling party's economic policy. This will also allow players to toggle Total Control, which bars capitalist pops from messing with factories, allowing for better micromanagement of industry.

This will enable state-wide core integration using the province selector. This takes 10 years to add the owner's cores and remove all other countries' cores from a state.\n\n§RThis will disable the regular RNG-based coring events.§!
This will enable extra infamy decay, granting an extra -0.05 infamy decay per month to all countries.
Enable receiving requests for grants from other players.
Disable receiving requests for grants from other players.
Send a request for money to other players who have grant requests enabled.

Total Control bars capitalist pops from building, expanding and re-opening factories, enabling you to better micromanage your industry. Unfortunately, infrastructure building is also affected.\n\n§GTotal Control can be toggled on and off at any time.§!
Disable Total Control, allowing capitalist pops to manage factories again.\n\n§GTotal Control can be toggled on and off at any time.§!
Raises your healthcare reform by one level, increasing your population growth, but also your spending.
Lowers your healthcare reform by one level, reducing your population growth and spending.
Raises your colonial migration reform by one level, increasing the amount of migrants your colonies get, but also burdening your bureaucracy, decreasing tax efficiency.
Lowers your colonial migration reform by one level, decreasing the amount of migrants your colonies get, but also unburdening your bureaucracy, increasing tax efficiency.
Choose an ideology to spread amongst your population, or end an influence campaign.

We can declare martial law. This will quell revolutionary elements within our nation, decreasing militancy and consciousness by §G-0.10§! per month. However, our economy will be negatively affected, decreasing our tax efficiency by §R-5.0%§!. Additionally, pop promotion will be hampered.
We can put an end to the state of martial law in our nation.
This will trigger an event for each one of our vassals in which we will be able to choose whether to release them or keep them as a vassal.
Starts a fake war with a non-existent country, which allows you to take control of your vassals' troops.
End the war with the fake target.

This will trigger an event that will allow you to ask other players that you are fighting to unoccupy land they've occupied from you or allow you to occupy their capital. This can be used to speed up the process of adding wargoals when a war has been won.\n\n§RDon't use this decision unless the enemy has surrendered.§!

We can leverage our political capital to force through (or rescind) a reform of our choice. This will upset the public and hurt the ruling party's popularity, and we are only able to do this once every 10 years.
